original_poem:
आकाशाचा पाय  
खोलातुन आवाज येतो,  
आभाळाच्या काठावर,  
धरणा धरून उभा आहे,  
आसमानातच आहे माणूस.
translated_poem:
Feet of the sky,  
A voice rises from deep,  
On the edge of the heavens,  
Standing firm,  
Humans dwell in the sky.

preprocessing.alignment_note: Source and translation stanza counts match.

translated_lines: On the edge of the heavens,

emotional_arc.value: A quiet, grounded wonder at human existence suspended between earth and sky, moving from depth to height.

translated_lines: On the edge of the heavens,

loss_note.value: धरणा धरून implies a protest-sit or stubborn holding-ground stance lost in 'standing firm'.

metaphor_spans.abstract_meaning: the sky's lowest reach touching human existence
humanity's stubborn, unyielding claim on its place in the cosmos
the threshold between mortal ground and infinite expanse
human existence placed within the cosmic expanse

visual_motifs.motif: a figure standing at the rim of the sky with feet planted on the edge of clouds
a figure standing firm at the edge of the sky
a voice rising from deep below
a voice rising upward from deep below the earth toward the heavens
humans dwelling within the open heavens
standing firm on the edge of the heavens

metaphor_spans.abstract_meaning: The grounding or foundation of the infinite cosmic expanse.
The threshold between the earthly realm and the celestial consciousness.
The realization that human existence is inherently part of the divine or infinite.
